title: When Life Gives You Tangerines — Jeju Route
drama: When Life Gives You Tangerines (당신, 거기 있어줄래요)
tip: Official filming locations per a March 2025 한국경제TV report: Seongsan Ilchulbong, Gimnyeong Beach, Jeju-mok Gwana, and Gasiri canola road. Rental car is not optional — most filming locations are inaccessible by public bus. Note: an earlier version of this route listed Udo Island — that was incorrect and has been removed; Udo does not appear in any verified filming-location coverage.
order_note: Day 1: Land at Jeju Airport → Jeju-mok Gwana (15 min from airport) → drive east to the Seongsan area for the night. Day 2: Seongsan Ilchulbong at sunrise → Seopjikoji (15 min drive) → Gasiri Noksan-ro canola road. Day 3: Gimnyeong Seonsegi Beach (haenyeo divers, best early morning) → back to the airport.

title: Goblin Route — Gangneung
transport: KTX from Seoul (1hr 30min). Local taxi recommended.
tip: KTX from Cheongnyangni or Seoul station direct to Gangneung. Note: Gangneung City Tour Bus service ended June 2025 — use taxi or Kakao Taxi. Gyeongpodae and Wolhwa Street were removed from this route — we could not verify a direct connection to Goblin filming and don't have confirmed coordinates yet.
order_note: Jumunjin Breakwater (30min from station by taxi, go 7-8am to avoid crowds) → Hyangho Beach BTS bus stop is a 5-min walk away, do both together. Free time after for Gangneung coffee street or Anmok Beach if you want more of the city.

title: Lovely Runner — Suwon Day Trip
transport: KTX 30min or Subway Line 1 direct from Seoul
tip: Café Monted (카페 몽테드) is confirmed as Im Sol's house exterior — about a 10-min walk from Hwaseong Fortress. Best budget day trip from Seoul. Note: Hwaseong Fortress itself isn't pinned on this map yet — we haven't independently verified coordinates for it, so treat it as a nearby UNESCO site to walk to, not a confirmed map point.
order_note: Arrive at Suwon Station → Café Monted for coffee (closed Wednesdays) → walk to Hwaseong Fortress and the Hwahongmun water gate area → Haenggung Palace → local 순대국밥 dinner.

title: BTS & HYBE — Seoul K-Pop Pilgrimage
transport: Subway only (all stops on major lines)
tip: HYBE building exterior has a designated ARMY photo zone at the main entrance. HYBE INSIGHT museum permanently closed in January 2023 — do not plan around visiting it, and ignore any guide that tells you to book tickets. Take the subway to Gangnam afterward, walk K-Star Road, and optionally continue to COEX Mall for K-pop merch shopping (not independently verified as a pinned stop).
order_note: Morning: HYBE building exterior photo zone (Yongsan) → lunch near Hangang → subway to Apgujeong → K-Star Road → optional: COEX Mall / Ktown4u for shopping.

title: SM & aespa — Seongsu Pilgrimage
tip: SM KWANGYA @ Seoul is walk-in, no reservation needed. Check @kwangya_official Instagram for current merch drops. Seongsu Yeongbang has rotating popup stores from Korean indie brands — check their Instagram before going since the lineup changes every 2-4 weeks. Note: we removed the 'NewJeans Bunny Mural' mention from an earlier version — couldn't confirm it's still there.
order_note: Seongsu Station → SM KWANGYA @ Seongsu → Seongsu Yeongbang for popups/cafes → explore the wider Seongsu cafe district.

title: Culinary Class Wars — Chef Restaurant Route
tip: These are alternatives, not a walking tour — pick one based on budget and how far ahead you can plan. Via Toledo (Chef Kwon Seong-jun, S1 winner, ₩19,000-23,000): reservations open 10th and 25th of each month at midnight on CatchTable, extremely competitive. Andongjip Son Kalguksu (budget, cash preferred): walk-in only in Gyeongdong Market. Mosu (Chef Ahn Sung-jae, ₩250,000-400,000 tasting menu): reserve 1st of each month, sells out in seconds. Note: Soigné was removed from this route — we found conflicting addresses (Gangnam-gu vs. Seocho-gu) between sources and couldn't resolve which is current before publishing.
order_note: Budget pick: Andongjip, walk-in only, go before 11:30am to avoid the queue. Mid-range: Via Toledo, reserve on CatchTable the 10th or 25th at midnight. Splurge: Mosu, reserve the 1st of the month, expect it to sell out in seconds.

title: Busan BTS & K-Pop Home Tour
transport: KTX from Seoul (2hrs 30min). Subway + taxi in Busan.
tip: Busan City Tour Bus: book on Klook (foreign card OK) — don't try to buy at the stop. ZM-ILLENNIAL Cafe is in Nam-gu, not Gwangalli — many maps show the wrong location (address verified: 부산 남구 진남로 135). Gamcheon's BTS mural is fan-made (2021), not official — HYBE has requested BTS structure removals elsewhere, so it could disappear; Gwangalli's BTS tie-in is a one-time 2022 concert, not a permanent site — go for the beach, treat BTS history as a bonus.
order_note: Day 1: Gamcheon Culture Village (morning) → Gwangalli Beach sunset. Day 2: ZM-ILLENNIAL Cafe → Haeundae Beach → optional: Busan Station area.
